title: phaseolus lunatus var. macrocarpus
intro: Phaseolus lunatus var. macrocarpus, also known as the large-seeded lima bean, is a variety of the Phaseolus lunatus plant, specifically grown for its large, flat seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. Consuming the large-seeded lima bean has been associated with various health benefits, including reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved digestion, and antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the seeds can be used to make teas, soups, or decoctions, which are believed to have therapeutic effects on the digestive system and overall well-being.
